channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: LA County reaches $4 billion agreement to settle sexual abuse claims at juvenile facilities
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Los Angeles County has reached a $4 billion agreement to settle nearly 7,000 claims of sexual abuse in juvenile facilities since 1959, officials said Friday.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. BLOOD and AMY TAXIN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Energy Department considers more than 40 percent of its staff non-essential as layoffs loom
item.description: The Energy Department's portfolio includes both energy technology and innovation as well as nuclear weapons.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WGN 9

item.title: Anaheim mayor wants Los Angeles Angels to change their name
item.description: In an open-letter about the Angels future, the Anaheim mayor called for the team to be named for the city it actually represents.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The US must return a Maryland man mistakenly deported to an El Salvador prison, a judge says
item.description: GREENBELT, Md. (AP) — A federal judge on Friday ordered the Trump administration to arrange for the return of a Maryland man to the United States after he was mistakenly deported to a notorious El Salvador prison, while a U.S. government attorney was at a loss to explain what happened.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN and BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump's trade war is decimating stock portfolios of the uber-rich -- for now
item.description: President Donald Trump's trade war with the world has seen trillions of dollars in wealth tied to the global markets evaporate within days. While all investors are feeling the pressure, experts say the wealthiest Americans are seeing perhaps the biggest impact.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: Washington and Oregon sue over Trump elections order, saying mail voters could be disenfranchised
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — Washington and Oregon on Friday became the latest states to ask a court to reject President Donald Trump’s efforts to overhaul elections in the U.S., a day after Democratic officials in 19 others filed a similar lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Dad's last-minute Christmas gift becomes $7M lotto winner
item.description: A California Lottery scratcher ticket that was given to a man as a gift by his father turned out to be a multimillion-dollar winner.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge blocks Trump admin's bid to move Tufts student case to Louisiana; sends it Vermont
item.description: A federal judge on Friday denied a Trump administration request to move a case involving a detained Tufts University doctoral student to Louisiana.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: TikTok deal pulled after Trump tariff announcement
item.description: A finalized TikTok deal was pulled Thursday after President Trump announced massive new tariffs against China, a source familiar with the negotiations told The Hill. Trump was poised to sign an executive order approving a deal that would have seen TikTok’s U.S.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Federal officials are quietly terminating the legal residency of some international college students
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A crackdown on foreign students is alarming college leaders, who say the Trump administration is using new tactics and vague justifications to push some students out of the country. College officials worry the new approach will keep foreigners from wanting to study in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, ANNIE MA and MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Interior secretary orders national parks to be open and accessible as workforce is cut
item.description: Under criticism for staff cuts across the country, Interior Secretary Doug Burgum is directing national parks to “remain open and accessible” and says officials will ensure proper staffing to do so.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Disgraced former Cardinal Theodore McCarrick has died at age 94
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Theodore McCarrick, a once-powerful Catholic cardinal who was defrocked by Pope Francis in 2019 after a Vatican investigation determined he had molested adults and children, has died. He was 94.
item.dc:creator: DAVID CRARY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Viral video shows Florida charter captain arguing with boater; police investigating
item.description: A viral video showing a fight between two Florida boaters has sparked both a police investigation and strong reactions from people online.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, Fox 5

item.title: Divided Supreme Court sides with Trump to block teacher grants
item.description: A divided Supreme Court sided with the Trump administration by allowing officials to block $65 million in teacher development grants frozen over concerns they were promoting diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) practices.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump to give TikTok another 75-day extension
item.description: President Trump said Friday that he plans to give TikTok another 75-day extension as his administration nears a deal to avert a ban on the popular social media platform.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, Fox 5

item.title: Sean 'Diddy' Combs hit with new sex trafficking charges a month before trial
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Federal prosecutors on Friday added two charges to Sean “Diddy” Combs ' indictment and said they expect four accusers to testify against him, expanding on allegations that the jailed hip-hop mogul engaged in sex trafficking with multiple women and as recently as last year.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: First private SpaceX polar mission splashes down near California
item.description: The first human SpaceX flight to orbit over Earth's north and south poles splashed down in the Pacific Ocean off the coast of Southern California on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US must return Maryland father mistakenly deported to El Salvador prison, judge rules
item.description: A federal judge on Friday ordered the Trump administration to arrange for the return of a Maryland man to the United States after he was mistakenly deported to a notorious El Salvador prison.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump approval slips to lowest point in second term: Survey
item.description: In the latest poll, 37 percent of respondents approved of Trump’s handling of the economy and 30 percent approved of his approach to addressing the high cost of living in the country.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: Transgender student's arrest for violating Florida bathroom law is thought to be a first
item.description: A transgender college student declared “I am here to break the law” before entering a women’s restroom at the Florida State Capitol and being led out in handcuffs by police.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: No more cheap skirts: Trump ends tax exemption for low-value Chinese imports
item.description: A notice to customers dazzled by the low-priced products on Chinese shopping apps: the days of getting trendy clothing, tools and gag gifts that cost less than lunch delivered to your door in 10 days are probably numbered.
item.dc:creator: ANNE D'INNOCENZIO and DIDI TANG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: White House postpones Saturday garden tours due to anti-Trump demonstrations
item.description: The White House is rescheduling one of its annual spring garden tour dates due to a "massive" anti-Trump demonstration.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: Deadly storms slash through the South and Midwest
item.description: The coming days were also forecast to bring the risk of potentially deadly flash flooding to the South and Midwest as severe thunderstorms blowing eastward become supercharged.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: When heavy rainfall threatens flooding, you can protect yourself. Here's how
item.description: Extreme weather is dangerous weather, and that's particularly true for the heavy rainfall events that experts say are becoming more frequent with climate change. The powerful storms can pose threats ranging from falling limbs to downed power lines to drowning.
item.dc:creator: ALEXA ST. JOHN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Some Republicans vote to reject President Trump’s Canada tariffs
item.description: On Wednesday, a group of Republican senators voted along with Democrats on a resolution to undo President Donald Trump's 25% tariffs on Canadian imports.
item.dc:creator: Reshad Hudson, Fox 5

item.title: Yankees OF Brett Gardner's son died of carbon monoxide poisoning, had 'layer' on organs, officials say
item.description: Randall Zúñiga, director of the Judicial Investigation Agency, said they tested the 14-year-old Miller Gardner for carboxyhemoglobin, a compound generated when carbon monoxide binds to hemoglobin in the blood.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Air traffic controllers to get more support after a fight and latest near miss at Washington airport
item.description: Air traffic controllers at Washington's Ronald Reagan National Airport will be offered crisis counseling and additional supervision after a fight in the tower and another alarming near miss two months after a midair collision between a passenger jet and an Army helicopter killed 67 people.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Final report details investigation into deadly 2023 Covenant School shooting
item.description: The Covenant School shooting report touches on the shooter's planning and the details unveiled during the investigation.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, Fox 5

item.title: Potential Trump tariff plan could cost households thousands: new analysis
item.description: A new analysis from The Budget Lab at Yale shows how much a broad tariff plan could cost U.S. households.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5

item.title: Nintendo announces Switch 2 release date, new features in latest Direct showcase
item.description: After years of waiting, Nintendo fans finally have a release date for the Japanese company's new gaming console.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, ABC 4

item.title: NFL to use camera technology to measure first downs next season, approves more rule changes
item.description: The NFL announced a multitude of rule changes on Tuesday, including a new way to measure first downs for the 2025 season.
item.dc:creator: Jared Bush, ABC 4

item.title: Amish buggies targeted in Pennsylvania robbery, attempted robbery
item.description: Some among Pennsylvania's Amish community are concerned that someone may be targeting them, after two buggies were held up at gunpoint, according to State Police.
item.dc:creator: Stan Boney, WGN 9

item.title: Most immigrants at risk of deportation from US are Christian, report finds
item.description: As many as four in five immigrants at risk of deportation from the United States are Christian, according to a new report that calls on their fellow believers to consider the impact of the Trump administration's aggressive deportation policies.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: April Fools' Day is celebrated with pranks and hoaxes worldwide
item.description: From France to Iceland to the United States, April Fools’ Day will be celebrated on Tuesday with practical jokes and elaborate hoaxes, so make sure to triple check viral posts and don’t leave your back open to any stray sticky notes.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Girl, 9, dies after procedure at California dental office
item.description: A report from the San Diego County Medical Examiner's Office claims the girl was discharged from the facility and remained asleep on the ride home.
item.dc:creator: Delaney White, WGN 9

item.title: Georgia lawmakers agree on school safety bill after Apalachee High School shooting
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Georgia lawmakers said repeatedly that a student-tracking database had been removed from a school safety bill, but parts of that plan survive.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump says he’s ‘not joking’ about a third term. Republicans insist he is
item.description: Congressional Republicans are brushing off President Trump’s latest comments about seeking a third term, downplaying them as a joke designed to needle the media even as the president insists he is serious.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, ABC 4
